steps.title: Read the passage for the contrasting examples
steps.content: The passage gives two musicians using the same “pay as you wish” model with opposite results: Harvey Danger earned below expectations (only ~1% of listeners paid), while Jane Siberry earned significantly because many listeners paid more than the suggested price. Any valid completion of the sentence must account for both outcomes.

steps.title: Eliminate each wrong choice, then confirm the match
steps.content:
• Choice A — 'prove financially successful for some musicians but disappointing for others': Directly captures the two contrasting examples — Siberry’s success and Harvey Danger’s disappointment. ✓
  • Choice B — 'hold greater financial appeal for bands than for individual musicians': Contradicts the examples; Harvey Danger (a band) underperformed while Siberry (an individual) succeeded. ✗
  • Choice C — 'cause most musicians who use the model to lower the suggested prices of their songs and albums over time': The passage never mentions musicians changing their prices. ✗
  • Choice D — 'more strongly reflect differences in certain musicians' popularity than traditional pricing models do': The text never compares “pay as you wish” to traditional pricing or discusses popularity. ✗
That matches choice A — A is correct.
